museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: What's this enormous GTA VI size whole we have this month?
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for May 2026. This is recent history, so dates and interpretation should be rechecked during human edit.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: May 2026 felt like a month defined as much by absence as by arrival. The delay of Grand Theft Auto VI continued to cast a long shadow over the industry, with publishers, players and commentators all trying to work out what a 2026 release calendar looked like without gaming's biggest event at its centre. At the same time, Forza Horizon 6 raced onto the scene as one of the year's most significant launches, while Nintendo's Switch 2 settled into its second year as a mainstream platform rather than a novelty. Across social media, gaming forums and streaming platforms, discussion increasingly centred on live-service updates, early-access roadmaps and long-term support plans, reflecting an industry where launch day was no longer the end of a game's story, but often just the beginning.

items.title: Directive 8020 and Indiana Jones on Switch 2 arrive
items.body: May 12th is a good day with the highly-anticipated Directive 8020 turning up, and Indy finally makes a crusade to the Switch 2. It wasn't all rosy for D8020, though, as it was leaked as a torrent 24 hours earlier.
items.artifactImageCaption: The game was caught up in a spate of high-profile leaks before launch.

items.title: Subnautica 2 enters early access on May 14.
items.body: After court cases and arguments, the world finally got its hands on Sub 2 - and it instantly sold four million copies and triggered the much-talked-about bonus payment to the one beleaguered devs.
items.artifactImageCaption: After all the drama, Subnautica 2 finally arrived in Early Access.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 launches in May for Xbox and PC.
items.body: The long-awaited Forza Horizon 6 launched, bringing Eastern charm with its racing around Tokyo and suburban Japan. It looked beautiful, but at 150mph the cherry blossom could have been anywhere.
items.artifactImageCaption: Forza Horizon 6 was another game that got leaked early, with devs blaming an early review copy. Bans were handed out to those playing early.

items.title: 007 First Light arrives on May 27.
items.body: The world wanted a bond game, and IO delivered in bucket loads. While not the longest game ever 007: First Light turned out to be fabulous, and not just a woke "British Hitman." with all the womanising removed so as not to upset people.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; recent-history claims should be rechecked during human edit.
items.coverImageAlt: An image of Bond from 007 First Light
items.artifactImageCaption: The least likely looking James Bond of all time. 007 First Light gave us a younger super spy guy.

items.title: GTA VI's former May 26 date becomes a visible delay marker, not a release.
items.body: With a new date of mid-November seemingly locked in, there was a lot of pondering to be done as to whether the game would see the light in 2026. Just one more small slip and another year will have passed us all by.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; recent-history claims should be rechecked during human edit.
items.artifactImageCaption: Where fore art thou Grand Theft Auto VI?

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: May's notable releases - a month packed full of action and great games - just no GTA VI.

items.title: Mixtape
items.body: On their last night together, three friends embark on one final adventure. Play through a mixtape of memories, set to the soundtrack of a generation.
items.platform: PC / PS5 / Xbox Series X|S / Switch 2

items.title: Directive 8020
items.body: Supermassive's Dark Pictures series goes into space.

items.title: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle
items.body: MachineGames' adventure reaches Nintendo's new hardware.

items.title: Subnautica 2
items.platform: PC / Xbox Series X|S early access
items.body: Underwater survival returns in a messy but closely watched early-access moment.
items.sourceNote: GamesRadar and Polygon/Gematsu references list May 14, 2026.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6
items.body: Playground's open-world racing series heads to Japan.
items.sourceNote: Gematsu and PC Gamer list May 19, 2026; GamesRadar reception page lists May 22.

items.title: 007 First Light
items.body: IO Interactive's Bond game arrives where GTA VI once sat on the calendar.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, subscription and buying context around the month.

items.title: Switch 2 enters its second calendar year
items.body: Ports, editions and Nintendo-first releases now test whether the new machine has become the main Switch console out there. May 8th saw the company announce a big price hike though with Nintendo apologising "for the impact these price revisions may have on our customers."

items.title: Hardware hikes
items.body: The demand for storage and RAM for AI data centres globally starts to put the squeeze on console prices with Sony, Nintendo and Valve ratcheting up huge hikes on the RRP. Don't anybody mention the word "profiteering.2

items.title: Xbox widens its platform map
items.body: Forza Horizon 6 launches on Xbox/PC with PS5 noted for later, underlining a less exclusive console landscape.
items.sourceNote: Gematsu and PC Gamer Forza reporting.

items.title: Xbox tries to get back in the fight
items.body: After getting beaten up recently, the new Xbox Gaming CEO starts punching back. The first baby steps, a new logo (XBOX) and a cut to the price of Game Pass - at the expense of losing Call of Duty games.

sections.title: Magazine Covers
sections.subtitle: Reconstructed shelf markers for print and digital cover culture.

items.headline: Edge on the 2026 shelf

items.title: PC Gamer UK
items.headline: PC Gamer UK on the 2026 shelf
items.curatorNote: Witchfire hit the cover and there was coverage inside of Vampire Crawlers and Windrose. Both huge games.
items.body: The PC-facing drawer for Marathon, Slay the Spire 2 early access, Forza Horizon 6, and the online calendar.

items.title: Retro Gamer
items.headline: Retro Gamer on the 2026 shelf
items.curatorNote: A very cool Death Star cover for a feature on Rogue Leader and Lego: The OG Trilogy. Plus a feature on the iconic ZX81/
items.body: Modern games keep recycling older names, remakes and archives; the past is still on the same shelf.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How players discovered, argued about and shared the month.

items.title: Delay culture is communal
items.body: GTA VI's missing May date becomes memes, mourning and calendar archaeology.

items.title: Early access is part of the main shelf
items.body: Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 show how unfinished-but-public games can still feel like major dates.

items.title: Platform identity is blurrier
items.body: Ports and cross-platform releases make the old console-war map harder to read.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A short atmospheric reading from the player side of recent history.

items.title: The year began under a shadow
items.body: Even before release, GTA VI shaped conversation like a planet just offstage. Any GTA VI story leapt upon by a desperate gaming media

items.title: Switch 2 became normal very quickly
items.body: While gaming journalism began to forgot about the OG Switch, players will still asking where the big games for the newbie console were. In truth, the only available answer... "not yet".

items.title: Hardware prices started to scare gamers
items.body: Huge hikes due due to 'global conditions' began to scare gamers who didn't want to pay an extra £200 for a Steam Deck.

items.title: May seems busy
items.body: With releases like Forza Horizon, 007 and Subnautica 2, May seemed much more active than normal for good games releases.
